message:
fix(runtime): JoinHandle::is_finished detects dropped executor side, trailing whitespace cleanup

JoinHandle::is_finished() now returns true when Arc::strong_count
on the shared state is 1, meaning the executor side has been
dropped without producing a result.  Previously is_finished() only
checked result.is_some(), so a JoinHandle whose task was
force-cancelled or whose runtime shut down would report
is_finished() = false forever.

This complements the existing poll-path detection (which panics on
the same condition) by making the non-async query path correct too.

Also removes a trailing whitespace line (cosmetic).

Test: join_handle_is_finished_after_executor_side_disappears drops
the Arc<Mutex<JoinState>> and verifies is_finished() returns true
and polling yields a panic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
